---
image: ../images/papers/2026-clinj-grading.png
title: "Fuzzy Semantic Retrieval Strategies for Automated Short-Answer Grading with Large Language Models in Language Learning"
subtitle: "Computational Linguistics in the Netherlands Journal, vol. 15, 2026"
description: "Adapts translation-memory fuzzy matching to automated grading, with an accuracy and recall trade-off by shot count."
author:
  - name: Thomas Moerman
  - name: Jasper Degraeuwe
  - name: Arda Tezcan
date: 2026-01-01
categories: [Educational NLP]
citation:
  type: article-journal
  container-title: "Computational Linguistics in the Netherlands Journal"
google-scholar: true
---

This work adapts translation-memory fuzzy matching to automated grading of short-answer language exercises. It evaluates ten retrieval strategies across 306 experiments and finds an accuracy and recall trade-off governed by the number of examples, with semantic retrieval generally beating non-semantic strategies.

Research theme: [Educational NLP](../research.qmd#educational-nlp)
